pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **Look, I get why people say Rafe's leading, but when your three draft picks didn't even qualify, calling it skill is kind of like calling lottery winners financial planners.** The Noisemaker is making some serious noise in this pool, leading the standings with 9 points, which might make one question the strategic acumen of the other veterans involved. I feel for Zach, I really do, but when you're relying on Saudi Arabia to eke out a draw with Uruguay, one has to wonder if you've confused persistence with a lack of foresight. Austria facing off against Jordan might seem like a gimme, but bless Mustard Boy's heart if he genuinely believes this matchup will secure his place off the bottom. Gayden, the Backpass Assassin, would like to think Jordan's plucky style can overcome Austria, but the only thing getting assassinated here might be his fantasy ambitions. Meanwhile, Bubba G has the rare pleasure of being both the winner and loser as France takes on Senegal, but hey, at least he won't be dropping any points. Gunner's dual-team selection practically guarantees he'll come away with something to brag about, but in all fairness, it takes a special kind of boldness to feel confident predicting your own tie game as a masterstroke.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe Leads Standings Despite Picking Three Non-Qualifying Teams
pundit_takes.subtitle: "The secret is in the chaos," Rafe insists from the top.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's Jordan and Austria Battle For Mid-Tier Supremacy
pundit_takes.subtitle: Backpass Assassin seeks glory by process of elimination.

pundit_takes.headline: Devin's Norway Poised To Shine Against Unpicked Iraq
pundit_takes.subtitle: Ghost Pepper expects solid points from Tier 2 calm.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner Sets New Fantasy Record With Self-Competing Teams
pundit_takes.subtitle: Bubba G's strategy is all about maximizing control.

rome_analytics_quotes: Zach and Gunner: the dynamic duo of not winning anything, sharing the bottom at 3 points.
Rafe drafted ghost teams, but the real joke's on Gunner relying totally on Morocco.
Devin's getting manhandled by a 15-year-old; he can't outscore Rafe's 6-point drubbing.
The sim said Devin would win, yet Rafe's chaos broke everyone's model faith.
